description: Local-first autonomous agent. Acts without being asked. Remembers without being reminded. Interrupts only for irreversible decisions.
qualityGates: cd core && cargo fmt --check && cargo clippy && cargo deny check && cargo test
ruff check && ruff format --check && mypy brain/src && pytest
cd web && npx tsc --noEmit && npm run lint && jest --runInBand

userStories.title: Shell Execution Engine
userStories.description: Rust-native shell execution engine with async streaming, cancellation, bounded timeouts, structured command model, and policy firewall admission control before any process is spawned.
acceptanceCriteria: Supports structured argv invocation (primary) and explicit-flag shell invocation (secondary)
Streaming stdout/stderr via tokio::process; output chunks delivered as they arrive — no buffering
Caller can cancel any in-flight process; SIGTERM sent, then SIGKILL after 5s grace period
Default timeout: 15 minutes; policy-extendable per command via VSH-003
Every execution records PID, start time, output reference for VSH-007 crash recovery
Command injection check on all inputs before FW classification — FW never sees raw untrusted input
sudo blocked unconditionally at this layer — not delegated to VSH-003
Executable not in allowlist rejected before FW classification; structured error returned
Firewall return contract respected: Tier 1 executes, Tier 2 holds until plan_token approved, Tier 3 always rejects
implementationPlan: Scaffold core/ Rust workspace: Cargo.toml, src/main.rs, src/shell/ module
Implement CommandDescriptor struct and structured argv vs shell invocation dispatch
Implement async process spawn via tokio::process with streaming stdout/stderr and cancellation
Implement allowlist check and command injection guard before firewall stub call
Add timeout enforcement (15-minute default) and SIGTERM/SIGKILL grace period
Implement execution record struct (PID, start time, output reference) and unit tests
userStories.technicalNotes: Phase 1 story. core/ is a Rust workspace. VSH-003 (Policy Firewall) is the admission gate — VSH-001 must call VSH-003 before spawning any process. Allowlist lives at ~/.vashion/policy/exec-allowlist.json. sudo is blocked at this layer unconditionally.
safetyGates: No process spawned before firewall returns allowed: true
Tier 2 pending state is caller-visible — SHELL never silently blocks
Tier 3 denial is final — no retry, no escalation
Shell invocation mode requires explicit flag — default is structured argv

userStories.title: Docker Lifecycle Integration
userStories.description: Rust-native Docker integration for managing local containers. All operations flow through the VSH-003 policy firewall with the same Tier 1/2/3 admission contract as VSH-001.
acceptanceCriteria: Supports: list, start, stop, build, inspect, logs against local Docker daemon
Streaming output contract identical to VSH-001
Caller can cancel any in-flight Docker operation; termination logged
Docker socket/group access preferred; --privileged blocked by default
Every Docker operation classified by VSH-003 firewall before execution
Destructive ops (prune, rm) classified as Tier 2 — require approval
All privilege violations audit-logged with action, tier, and reason
implementationPlan: Add src/docker/ module to core/ workspace
Implement DockerClient wrapping bollard or shelling to docker CLI with explicit socket path
Implement operation enum (list, start, stop, build, inspect, logs) with ActionDescriptor construction
Wire every operation through VSH-003 firewall before execution
Classify prune and rm as Tier 2 in ActionDescriptor (reversibility=irreversible)
Add cancellation and audit logging; unit tests for each operation type
userStories.technicalNotes: Depends on VSH-003 being scaffolded first. --privileged must be blocked at descriptor construction, not left to policy config. All operations use same Tier plan_token contract as VSH-001.
safetyGates: --privileged blocked by default; requires explicit policy exception
docker prune and docker rm are Tier 2 — no auto-execution

userStories.title: Policy Firewall
userStories.description: Deterministic policy firewall classifying every action via machine-verifiable ActionDescriptor schema into Tier 1 (auto-execute), Tier 2 (approval required), or Tier 3 (blocked). Soul.md and Behavior.md tune policy — they do not replace the classifier.
acceptanceCriteria: Every action classified via ActionDescriptor — missing or invalid descriptor is a hard rejection
TierDecision written to audit log before execution for every action
Tier 1: plan_token returned, execution proceeds immediately
Tier 2: plan_token held; AutoApprovalPolicy evaluated first; auto-approve or escalate to user
Tier 3: immediate structured rejection; audit log entry written; no bypass path
Policy allowlist at ~/.vashion/policy/allowlist.json — user-editable; every change audit-logged
Core authenticates every outbound /approvals webhook with session token
implementationPlan: Define ActionDescriptor schema as a Rust struct with all 8 required fields
Implement deterministic tier classification rules (Tier 1/2/3) as pure functions against descriptor
Implement AutoApprovalPolicy: deny-by-default allowlist, decision record with hash+nonce+TTL
Implement plan_token lifecycle: issue on Tier 1/2, resolve on approval, reject on Tier 3
Implement /approvals outbound webhook to Brain with Bearer token auth and 3-retry backoff
Write TierDecision to audit log before execution; add unit tests for each tier path
userStories.technicalNotes: This is the critical shared gate used by VSH-001, VSH-002, and VSH-004. Tier rules are evaluated against descriptor fields, not prose. decision_hash ties approval to exact action parameters — modified action requires new approval. Nonce is single-use; TTL default 30s.
safetyGates: Descriptor fields are required — missing field = Tier 2 minimum; unknown fields never default to Tier 1
Auto-approval allowlist is deny-by-default — unlisted action classes always escalate to user
Decision hash enforced — modified action cannot reuse prior approval
Tier 3 has no bypass — not even with explicit session-level approval
No approval request is silently lost — undelivered requests sit in pending-delivery queue until Brain reconnects

userStories.title: Policy-Bounded File Operations
userStories.description: File system operations scoped to configured workspace roots with policy tier enforcement via VSH-003.
acceptanceCriteria: Supports: read, write, delete, move, list within configured workspace roots
Workspace roots defined in ~/.vashion/config.toml
Reads within roots: Tier 1. Writes within roots: Tier 1. Operations outside roots: Tier 2.
delete is always Tier 2 — irreversible, regardless of path
Symlink traversal blocked by default; must not escape workspace root
Every file operation audit-logged with path, operation, tier, and outcome
implementationPlan: Add src/files/ module to core/ workspace
Implement workspace root configuration reader from ~/.vashion/config.toml
Implement path canonicalization and symlink escape detection before ActionDescriptor construction
Implement read/write/delete/move/list operations with correct tier assignment per operation and path
Wire all operations through VSH-003 firewall
Audit-log every operation with path, tier, and outcome; add unit tests including symlink escape cases
userStories.technicalNotes: delete is always Tier 2 regardless of path — do not make this configurable. Symlink escape detection must run before firewall classification. Workspace roots from ~/.vashion/config.toml.
safetyGates: Symlink escape detection on every path before FW classification — FW never sees an escaped path
Delete always Tier 2 — no exception

userStories.title: Auth & Model Registry
userStories.description: Encrypted credential storage and model registry for multi-provider LLM support. Manages Core/Brain bootstrap auth with explicit token epoch and rekey handshake for Core restarts mid-session.
acceptanceCriteria: Stores model credentials encrypted at rest at ~/.vashion/auth/
Supports providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, local (Ollama)
Active model switchable at runtime without restart
Credentials never written to logs, memory DB, or audit trail — appear as [REDACTED]
Model registry stores per-model: name, provider, context window, cost tier
Registry queried by brain at startup to inform goal loop and context budgeting
Auth state included in VSH-007 checkpoint so model context survives crashes
Rekey handshake completes without manual intervention — Brain self-recovers from Core restart
implementationPlan: Implement session token generation and epoch-stamped write to ~/.vashion/auth/core.token (mode 600)
Implement token validation on every Unix socket connection: check token AND epoch
Implement rekey protocol: Core restart increments epoch, Brain detects mismatch, enters degraded state, reloads token
Implement encrypted credential store for Anthropic/OpenAI/Ollama with [REDACTED] log filter
Implement model registry struct (name, provider, context_window, cost_tier) with runtime switch support
Add auth state to checkpoint struct for VSH-007 integration
userStories.technicalNotes: Token format: { token, epoch, issued_at, ttl }. Wrong token OR stale epoch = immediate rejection. Brain enters degraded state on epoch mismatch, not just auth failure. Credentials must never appear in any log, memory, or audit entry.
safetyGates: Credentials never logged — enforced at write layer, not caller convention
Registry query is read-only; writes require explicit mutation call
Stale epoch rejection is immediate — Brain never retries with a known-bad token
Degraded state is operator-visible via Canvas health endpoint

userStories.title: Heartbeat & Event Bus
userStories.description: Continuous environment monitoring with delta classification (routine/notable/urgent) and push-based event delivery to brain via authenticated HTTP POST webhook.
acceptanceCriteria: Runs on configurable interval (default: 60s; env: VASHION_HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL)
On every pulse: snapshot processes, containers, disk, memory, network activity
Diffs snapshot against previous; classifies each delta: routine / notable / urgent
Routine: logged locally only. Notable: posted to brain /events with traceparent. Urgent: posted immediately outside pulse schedule.
Pulse history retained for 24h; entries older than 24h pruned automatically
Static urgent thresholds always active: mandatory service down 2 pulses, disk > 85%, mem < 512MB
Z-score anomaly detection with 30-sample minimum, 2σ threshold, burst suppression, 5-min dedup cooldown
Webhook delivery retried up to 3 times with exponential backoff; failure written to audit log
implementationPlan: Add src/heartbeat/ module; implement configurable pulse scheduler (tokio interval)
Implement snapshot collector: processes, containers, disk, memory, network
Implement delta classifier: diff against previous snapshot, apply static thresholds and z-score model
Implement escalation state machine per resource (normal → warning → urgent → sustained_urgent)
Implement Brain webhook delivery with Bearer token auth, 3-retry backoff, and urgent local queue on failure
Implement 24h pulse history with automatic pruning; add tests for threshold breach and dedup cooldown
userStories.technicalNotes: Urgent events bypass pulse schedule — fire immediately. Cooldown does not apply to static threshold breaches. Z-score activates only after 30 samples; before that, fall back to static cold-start thresholds (CPU > 90%, mem < 256MB).
safetyGates: Webhook delivery retried; failure written to audit log
Urgent events never dropped — written to local urgent queue if webhook fails
Cooldown does not apply to static threshold breaches

userStories.title: Checkpoint & Crash Recovery
userStories.description: Checkpoint-based state persistence for crash recovery. Surfaces dirty state to brain on restart — does not auto-resume.
acceptanceCriteria: Checkpoint written before every Tier 2 operation and on clean shutdown (SIGTERM/SIGINT)
Checkpoint contains: active processes, open Docker operations, pending Tier 2 approvals, auth state reference, heartbeat cursor
On startup: detect prior checkpoint file; classify as clean or dirty
dirty checkpoint (prior crash): posted to Brain event bus as urgent event before accepting new commands
clean checkpoint: loaded silently; state restored, Brain notified
Checkpoint files at ~/.vashion/checkpoints/; rotate — keep last 5, delete older
Recovery does not auto-resume interrupted operations — reports state and waits for direction
implementationPlan: Define Checkpoint struct covering all required state fields
Implement atomic checkpoint write (write to temp file, then rename) at SIGTERM/SIGINT and pre-Tier-2
Implement startup checkpoint detection: classify clean vs dirty, surface dirty as urgent event via VSH-006
Implement checkpoint rotation: keep last 5, prune older on each write
Integrate auth state reference from VSH-005 into checkpoint struct
Add unit tests for atomic write, rotation, clean vs dirty classification
userStories.technicalNotes: Checkpoint write is atomic (write to temp, rename) — no partial checkpoint files. Checkpoint files never written to MEM_S — disk only, so recovery is independent of database state. Recovery never auto-resumes.
safetyGates: Checkpoint write is atomic (write to temp, rename) — no partial checkpoint files
Dirty checkpoint always surfaced before new input accepted
Checkpoint files never written to MEM_S — disk only

userStories.title: Memory Store
userStories.description: Two-tier memory storage (episodic and durable) in SQLite. Single internal async write queue. Brain accesses exclusively via Unix socket memory API — no direct DB connection from Brain.
acceptanceCriteria: Two stores: episodic (short-term, 7-day default expiry) and durable (persisted across sessions)
Episodic entries: timestamp, source service, content, relevance score, expiry
Durable entries: promoted from episodic by brain only; require user_approved = true
Context budget enforced at item granularity — whole items dropped when over budget, never truncated
Memory API exposed on Unix socket alongside other core commands
Brain accesses MEM_S exclusively via Unix socket memory API — no direct DB connection
Pending Tier 2 plan_token records stored in MEM_S; never silently expire
On Brain reconnect: Brain queries memory_query { filter: approvals:pending } to re-surface pending approvals
implementationPlan: Create SQLite schema at ~/.vashion/memory.db (WAL mode): episodic and durable tables, approval_state table
Implement single async write queue (tokio channel) for all writes; tag source: core | brain on every entry
Implement memory API over Unix socket: read, write, query, promote, expire operations
Implement context budget enforcement: drop lowest-relevance items at item granularity
Implement episodic 7-day expiry and durable promotion guard (user_approved required)
Implement approval state schema and reconnect reconciliation path; add integration tests
userStories.technicalNotes: SQLite at ~/.vashion/memory.db with WAL mode. Single internal async write queue — no lock contention, no last-write-wins. Durable promotion requires user_approved=true enforced in write queue, not caller convention. Brain has no direct SQLite access.
safetyGates: Durable write requires user_approved = true — enforced in write queue, not caller convention
Context budget enforcement is a hard error — not a warning
Brain has no direct SQLite access — all reads and writes through Unix socket API
Pending approval state lives in Core — not in any process that can be restarted without losing it

userStories.title: LLM Gateway via MCP
userStories.description: Python MCP server exposing core capabilities as structured tools for LLM consumption. Single Unix socket client for all Brain→Core communication. Handles three distinct failure modes with defined behavior for each.
acceptanceCriteria: Implements MCP server exposing core as tools: shell_exec, docker_op, file_read, file_write, memory_query, heartbeat_status
Every tool call follows: LLM → Brain (MCP) → Core (Unix socket + session token); traceparent propagated at every hop
Per-call Unix socket timeout: 30s default — no call waits indefinitely
Three failure modes handled distinctly: connection refused, auth rejection, timeout — each with defined Brain response
LLM failure mode: retry once → fallback chain (Anthropic → OpenAI → Ollama) → halt
Cost logged per call: model name, tokens in, tokens out, duration ms
MEM_I and HB_CTX route all Core memory queries through this layer
implementationPlan: Scaffold brain/ Python package with src/main.py entry point and MCP server setup
Implement Unix socket client with session token + epoch auth and 30s per-call timeout
Implement three failure mode handlers: connection refused → degraded, auth rejection → rekey, timeout → degraded
Implement MCP tool definitions: shell_exec, docker_op, file_read, file_write, memory_query, heartbeat_status
Implement LLM fallback chain (Anthropic → OpenAI → Ollama) with cost logging per call
Add traceparent propagation on all hops; add unit tests for each failure mode
userStories.technicalNotes: Depends on Phase 1 (VSH-001 through VSH-008) complete. Brain is the sole Unix socket client — MEM_I and HB_CTX use this layer, not direct socket access. Degraded state: no new goal acceptance. Recovery: poll socket every 10s, exit degraded on successful ping.
safetyGates: Session token validated on every Unix socket connection
traceparent required on all tool calls — missing header is an error
Costs logged before result returned — no silent failures
Fallback chain exhausted = hard halt, not silent degradation
No Unix socket call waits indefinitely — 30s timeout enforced without exception

userStories.title: Goal Loop
userStories.description: Continuous planning and execution loop. Receives goals from web REST API, heartbeat urgent events, and internal scheduler. Manages priority queue, resource locks, preemption, and Core-unreachable behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Goals sourced from: web REST API, heartbeat urgent events (VSH-012), internal scheduler
Each goal tracked with status: queued / active / blocked / complete / failed / preempted
Priority queue: urgent > operator > scheduled; urgent goals preempt operator goals
Resource lock model: workspace, container, system — acquire all or none, atomic
Blocked goals (awaiting Tier 2 approval) hold locks released; locks re-acquired on resume
Loop reads Soul.md and Behavior.md at startup — missing file is a hard error
Core unreachable: Goal Loop halts immediately, active goals fail or block, blocked goals re-queued on recovery
Failed goals: root cause written to episodic memory; no blind retry
implementationPlan: Implement priority queue with urgent/operator/scheduled tiers and preemption semantics
Implement resource lock model (workspace/container/system): acquire-all-or-none, atomic
Implement goal lifecycle state machine: queued → active → blocked/complete/failed/preempted
Load Soul.md and Behavior.md at startup; hard error on missing file
Implement Core-unreachable handler: halt acceptance, transition active goals, re-queue on recovery
Write goal history, reasoning steps, lock events to episodic memory via VSH-009; add tests
userStories.technicalNotes: Depends on VSH-009. Starvation prevention: scheduled goals starved > 10 minutes promoted to operator priority. Urgent cancellation can cancel any active goal; operator cancellation limited to operator and scheduled goals only.
safetyGates: Soul.md / Behavior.md loaded at startup — missing file is a hard error
Lock acquisition is atomic — partial lock is not held
No blind retry — failure written to memory, diagnosis required
Preempted operator goals re-queued, never silently dropped
Core unreachable = Goal Loop halts immediately

userStories.title: Memory & Context Intelligence
userStories.description: Relevance scoring, auto-promotion pipeline, and session-start context assembly. Brain intelligence layer over the raw MEM_S store. Accesses MEM_S exclusively via VSH-009 Unix socket API.
acceptanceCriteria: Reads episodic entries from MEM_S via VSH-009 Unix socket memory API
Relevance scoring: ranks episodic entries for inclusion in LLM context at goal loop startup
Promotion follows 6-stage pipeline — no stage may be skipped
Session-start narrative: synthesizes last heartbeat delta + durable context before first goal
Context budget: respects VSH-008 limits; lowest-relevance items dropped first
Durable entries immutable after write; updates via deprecation chain only
Soft-delete with 30-day retention; hard-delete after 30 days; deletion event to audit log
implementationPlan: Implement relevance scoring model with tunable weights from ~/.vashion/memory-config.toml
Implement 6-stage durable promotion pipeline: candidate detection, normalization, dedup check, operator review bundle, immutable approval record, durable write with provenance
Implement session-start narrative synthesis (last heartbeat delta + durable context briefing)
Implement context budget assembly: rank by relevance, drop lowest-relevance items at item granularity
Implement durable lifecycle: immutable writes, deprecation chain for updates, soft/hard delete
Add tests for promotion pipeline stage enforcement and dedup rejection
userStories.technicalNotes: Depends on VSH-009. No direct SQLite access — all reads and writes through VSH-009 Unix socket API. Session-start narrative failure is non-fatal but logged. Promotion candidate detection: episodic entry referenced in 3+ distinct sessions.
safetyGates: No direct SQLite access — all reads and writes through VSH-009 Unix socket API
No silent durable promotion — all 6 stages required
Duplication check is mandatory — near-duplicate promotion is a hard rejection
Durable entries are immutable — updates via deprecation chain only

userStories.title: Heartbeat Context Awareness
userStories.description: Consumes core heartbeat events, maintains ambient situational awareness, routes classified events to Goal Loop (urgent) and MEM_I (notable). Produces session-start narrative from last 24h heartbeat history.
acceptanceCriteria: Exposes webhook endpoint at Brain entry point /events for core heartbeat push
Classifies each event against Soul.md / Behavior.md decision framework
Notable events: forwarded to MEM_I for enriched episodic write
Urgent events: inserted as priority-0 goal in VSH-010 immediately; also forwarded to MEM_I
Session-start: loads last 24h heartbeat narrative into context before accepting user input
If brain was offline when core posted: event written to core local urgent queue; delivered on reconnect
HB_CTX never writes directly to MEM_S — all memory writes via MEM_I → VSH-009
implementationPlan: Implement /events webhook endpoint in Brain entry point
Implement event classifier against Soul.md / Behavior.md rules
Route notable events to MEM_I for episodic write; route urgent events to Goal Loop as priority-0
Implement session-start heartbeat narrative loader from MEM_I (last 24h)
Implement while-you-were-away section for session-start when notable events occurred during gap
Add tests for routing, narrative generation, and offline delivery path
userStories.technicalNotes: Depends on VSH-010 and VSH-011. HB_CTX never writes directly to MEM_S — all writes go through MEM_I → VSH-009. Urgent events never silently dropped — delivery to Goal confirmed or event queued.
safetyGates: Urgent events never silently dropped — delivery to GOAL confirmed or event queued
Session-start narrative includes explicit while-you-were-away section if notable events occurred
HB_CTX never writes directly to MEM_S

userStories.title: Canvas Backend
userStories.description: REST API backend at 127.0.0.1:7474 serving the Canvas UI and proxying all commands to brain. Single validated entry point for all external requests including plugin webhooks.
acceptanceCriteria: Endpoints: goal submission, goal status, heartbeat summary, memory query, system status, plugin webhook receiver
Generates request_id (UUID v4) at request entry; propagated through all downstream calls
Proxies all requests to Brain at 127.0.0.1:7475 with traceparent and request_id
Plugin webhooks (Slack, Telegram) auth token validated before forwarding to Brain
All requests and responses logged via pino with full shared log schema
Health check endpoint at /health — returns Core and Brain reachability status
implementationPlan: Scaffold web/ TypeScript package: tsconfig, package.json, src/main.ts entry point
Implement Express/Fastify REST server at 127.0.0.1:7474 with structured logging via pino
Implement request_id generation (UUID v4) and traceparent injection at entry
Implement proxy middleware for all Brain-bound requests with auth forwarding
Implement plugin webhook auth validation (token check before any forwarding)
Implement /health endpoint querying Core and Brain reachability; add integration tests
userStories.technicalNotes: Depends on Phase 2 (VSH-009 through VSH-012) complete. traceparent generated at entry if not present; never silently dropped. All external calls to Brain wrapped with explicit error handling. Core→Brain webhook calls on /approvals and /events must validate session token.
safetyGates: traceparent generated at entry if not present; never silently dropped
Plugin webhook auth validated before any forwarding — unauthenticated plugin requests rejected 401
All external calls to Brain wrapped with explicit error handling

userStories.title: Canvas Frontend
userStories.description: Single-page local-first UI for monitoring and interacting with Vashion. Served by VSH-013 backend. No external dependencies.
acceptanceCriteria: Served by VSH-013 backend at 127.0.0.1:7474
Goals panel: active goals with status, step trace, and result
Heartbeat panel: live event feed with routine/notable/urgent classification
Memory panel: episodic memory timeline; durable memory browser
System panel: disk, memory, container status at a glance
REPL panel: raw command input with Tier classification displayed before execution
Real-time updates via WebSocket (VSH-015)
Tier 2 approval prompts surface in UI — user approves or denies inline
No external dependencies — no CDN calls; all assets served locally
implementationPlan: Implement SPA structure served from VSH-013; configure bundler with no external CDN assets
Implement Goals panel with real-time status updates via WebSocket
Implement Heartbeat panel with event classification display
Implement Memory panel: episodic timeline and durable browser
Implement System panel: disk/mem/container status
Implement REPL panel with Tier classification preview and Tier 2 inline approval/denial UI
userStories.technicalNotes: Depends on VSH-013 and VSH-015. All assets must be served locally — no CDN. Tier 2 approvals require explicit user action in UI — no auto-approval. Approval state is owned by Core (VSH-008), not Web process memory.
safetyGates: Tier 2 approvals require explicit user action — no auto-approval in UI
No external network calls from frontend

userStories.title: WebSocket Bridge
userStories.description: WebSocket server at 127.0.0.1:7474/ws providing real-time bidirectional communication. All incoming client messages route through Canvas Backend validation — no separate unvalidated entry point.
acceptanceCriteria: WebSocket server at 127.0.0.1:7474/ws
Pushes to client: goal state changes, heartbeat events, Tier 2 approval requests, urgent alerts
Receives from client: goal submission, Tier 2 approval/denial, REPL commands
All incoming WS messages routed through Canvas Backend request validation before reaching Brain
traceparent generated at WS message receipt; propagated on all downstream calls
Client auto-reconnects with exponential backoff (max 30s interval)
Messages typed and validated on receipt — malformed messages logged and discarded
implementationPlan: Implement WebSocket server on 127.0.0.1:7474/ws alongside VSH-013 REST server
Implement message routing: all incoming WS messages through Canvas Backend validation pipeline
Implement server-push channels: goal state, heartbeat events, Tier 2 approval prompts, urgent alerts
Implement client-side reconnect with exponential backoff (max 30s)
Implement typed message schema validation; log and discard malformed messages
Add tests for validation pipeline, disconnect handling, and approval state recovery on reconnect
userStories.technicalNotes: Depends on VSH-013. WS messages share the same validation and tracing pipeline as REST — no free pass. Pending approval state owned by Core (VSH-008) — disconnect and backend restart do not lose it; Web re-fetches on reconnect. Rate limiting applied equally to WS and REST.
safetyGates: WS messages share the same validation and tracing pipeline as REST
Pending approval state owned by Core — disconnect does not lose approval state
Rate limiting applied equally to WS and REST paths

userStories.title: Slack Plugin
userStories.description: Goal submission and status updates via Slack DM. Urgent alerts pushed proactively. Plugin webhook received by VSH-013, auth token validated, forwarded to Brain. Deferred — build after Phase 3 stable.
acceptanceCriteria: Goal submission via Slack DM forwarded through VSH-013 Canvas Backend
Status updates pushed to Slack on goal completion or failure
Urgent alerts pushed proactively to configured Slack channel
Plugin webhook auth validated at VSH-013 before any forwarding to Brain
Plugin host process shares Brain auth context
implementationPlan: Implement Slack plugin handler in brain/plugins/ within the single plugin host process
Implement Slack webhook receiver registration and token validation
Implement goal submission path: Slack DM → VSH-013 → Brain Goal Loop
Implement proactive push: Brain urgent alert → Slack DM/channel
Add end-to-end tests for submit, status update, and alert paths
userStories.technicalNotes: Deferred until Phase 3 complete and verified. Plugin host is a single process in brain/plugins/ sharing Brain auth context. All plugin-originated requests enter via VSH-013 Canvas Backend — no direct Brain access.
safetyGates: Plugin webhook auth validated at VSH-013 before any forwarding
No direct Brain Goal Loop access from plugin — must go through Canvas Backend

userStories.title: Telegram Plugin
userStories.description: Goal submission and urgent alerts via Telegram bot. Same entry path as VSH-016 — through VSH-013 Canvas Backend. Deferred — build after Phase 3 stable.
acceptanceCriteria: Goal submission via Telegram bot forwarded through VSH-013 Canvas Backend
Urgent alerts pushed proactively to configured Telegram chat
Plugin webhook auth validated at VSH-013 before any forwarding to Brain
Plugin host process shares Brain auth context
implementationPlan: Implement Telegram plugin handler in brain/plugins/ within the single plugin host process
Implement Telegram bot webhook receiver and token validation
Implement goal submission path: Telegram → VSH-013 → Brain Goal Loop
Implement proactive push: Brain urgent alert → Telegram chat
Add end-to-end tests for submit and alert paths
userStories.technicalNotes: Deferred until Phase 3 complete and verified. Same architecture as VSH-016 — single plugin host process, all requests through Canvas Backend.
safetyGates: Plugin webhook auth validated at VSH-013 before any forwarding
No direct Brain Goal Loop access from plugin — must go through Canvas Backend
